Tests if the Number object has the property "NEGATIVE_INFINITY".

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks if the `Number` object in JavaScript has a property named `NEGATIVE_INFINITY`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the `NEGATIVE_INFINITY` property exists on the `Number` object, which is a standard property in JavaScript representing negative infinity. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on the presence of this specific property within the `Number` object. It is likely used in a testing context to ensure that the JavaScript environment correctly implements this part of the ECMAScript specification.
